description: Catechism of the Catholic Church - Key Paragraphs for Daily Reflections

prayer.text: Prayer is the raising of one's mind and heart to God or the requesting of good things from God.
Jesus prays and teaches us to pray. His prayer shows us how to pray to our Father.
Pray at all times with thanksgiving. Rejoice always, pray constantly, give thanks in all circumstances.
Blessing expresses the basic movement of Christian prayer: it is an encounter between God and man.
When we pray for the needs of others, we intercede. Abraham, Moses, and the prophets interceded for the people.

faith.text: By faith, man completely submits his intellect and will to God. With his whole being man gives his assent to God who reveals.
Faith is necessary for salvation. The Lord himself affirms: He who believes and is baptized will be saved.
Faith is a personal act and also ecclesial. We believe because the Church believes.
Faith is the theological virtue by which we believe in God and believe all that he has said and revealed to us.

love.text: Charity is the theological virtue by which we love God above all things for his own sake, and our neighbor as ourselves for the love of God.
The fruits of charity are joy, peace, and mercy. Charity edifies and bears much fruit.
The fruits of the Spirit are perfections that the Holy Spirit forms in us as the first fruits of eternal glory.
The fourth commandment illuminates relationships in society. It speaks of our duties to family and community.

hope.text: Hope is the theological virtue by which we desire the kingdom of heaven and eternal life as our happiness.
Christian hope unfolds from the beginning of Jesus' preaching in the proclamation of the beatitudes.
We can therefore hope in the glory of heaven promised by God to those who love him and do his will.

suffering.text: Illness can lead to anguish and self-absorption, sometimes even to despair and revolt against God.
Union with the passion of Christ. By the grace of this sacrament the sick person receives the strength and gift of uniting himself more closely to Christ's Passion.
The way of perfection passes by way of the Cross. There is no holiness without renunciation and spiritual battle.
God permits evil in order to draw forth some greater good. Out of the greatest moral evil, God brought the greatest good: the redemption.

forgiveness.text: This 'as' is not unique in Jesus' teaching. Our prayer is answered only if we ourselves have forgiven first.
Christian prayer extends to the forgiveness of enemies, transfiguring the disciple by configuring him to his Master.
There is no offense, however serious, that the Church cannot forgive. There is no one so wicked that Christ does not offer salvation.
This mercy is not merely a word. It is the love that goes beyond measure, the love that forgives.

eucharist.text: The Eucharist is the source and summit of the Christian life. All other sacraments are oriented to the Eucharist.
In the most blessed sacrament of the Eucharist the body and blood, together with the soul and divinity, of our Lord Jesus Christ is truly, really, and substantially contained.
By the consecration the transubstantiation of the bread and wine into the Body and Blood of Christ is brought about.

mary.text: The Virgin Mary is acknowledged and honored as being truly the Mother of God and of the Redeemer.
Mary's role in the Church is inseparable from her union with Christ and flows directly from it.
In prayer we unite ourselves to the heart of Mary, as she pondered these things in her heart.
Called in the Gospels 'the Mother of Jesus', Mary is acclaimed by Elizabeth as 'the mother of my Lord'.

saints.text: By canonizing some of the faithful, the Church proclaims that they practiced heroic virtue and lived in fidelity to God's grace.
Communion with the saints joins us to Christ, from whom as from its fountain and head issues all grace and all life.
The intercession of the saints. Being more closely united to Christ, those who dwell in heaven fix the whole Church more firmly in holiness.

scripture.text: The Church has always venerated the divine Scriptures just as she venerates the body of the Lord.
In Sacred Scripture the Church finds constant nourishment and strength. She receives with joy the bread of life.
And such is the force and power of the Word of God that it can serve the Church as her support and vigor.
Through all the words of Sacred Scripture, God speaks only one single Word, his one Utterance in whom he expresses himself fully.

church.text: The Church is the People of God. God does not belong exclusively to any one people.
The Church was prepared in the Old Covenant and instituted by Christ through his death and resurrection.
This is the sole Church of Christ, which in the Creed we profess to be one, holy, catholic and apostolic.

mercy.text: The Gospel is the revelation in Jesus Christ of God's mercy to sinners.
This mercy is not merely a word. It is the love that goes beyond measure.
God's almighty power is in no way arbitrary. It is the power of a Father who loves his children.
If we refuse to forgive, our prayer cannot be heard. God's mercy cannot penetrate our closed heart.

grace.text: Grace is favor, the free and undeserved help that God gives us to respond to his call.
Sanctifying grace is a habitual gift, a stable and supernatural disposition that perfects the soul itself.
Sanctifying grace makes us pleasing to God and enables us to live as his children.

peace.text: Respect for and development of human life require peace. Peace is not merely the absence of war.
Peace is the work of justice and the effect of charity. It requires respect for human dignity.
Love of country and responsibility for the common good are noble sentiments that promote peace.

family.text: The Christian family is a communion of persons, a sign and image of the communion of the Father and the Son.
The Christian family constitutes a specific revelation and realization of ecclesial communion.
Parents have the first responsibility for the education of their children in the faith.

default.text: God, infinitely perfect and blessed in himself, created man out of sheer goodness to share in his blessed life.
The desire for God is written in the human heart, because man is created by God and for God.
Prayer is the raising of one's mind and heart to God.

url_format: Double underscore format (__P{section}.HTM) for Vatican archive pages
